summary: End-to-end frontend testing of 3 new faith features + regression. Prayer Corner, God's Promises, and all regressions PASS. ONE CRITICAL BUG: badge-unlock confetti never appears because KidHome.js never calls setCelebrate(true). Backend behavior is correct (first mood awards 2 stars, first_step badge granted), and the Confetti component itself renders correctly when show=true, but the trigger logic in pickMood() is missing.

ui_bugs.issue: Badge-unlock confetti NEVER triggers. setCelebrate(true) is not called anywhere in KidHome.js. After a fresh child's first mood check-in (stars 0->2, first_step badge granted by backend), zero confetti emoji spans appear in DOM. The <Confetti show={celebrate}/> overlay is wired up but the celebrate state is always false. Required fix: in pickMood, compare child.badges.length before vs new badges in response (data.child.badges.length); if it increased, setCelebrate(true). Example: const before=(child?.badges||[]).length; const newCount=(data.child?.badges||[]).length; if (newCount>before) setCelebrate(true); setChild(data.child);

action_items: Fix KidHome.js pickMood to call setCelebrate(true) when badges count increases (e.g., crossing 1/10/25/50/100 star thresholds). Backend already returns updated child with badges array; just diff old vs new length.
critical_code_review_comments: KidHome.js line 42-48: pickMood() never invokes setCelebrate. The Confetti overlay on line 54 is effectively dead code until this is fixed.
PrayerCorner.js: PROMPTS contain spaces -> data-testid like 'prayer-prompt-Thank You God for...' which is fine for tests but unconventional; consider slugifying.
Promises.js: useEffect uses categories[0] as initial active; this is correct so default verses render on load (verified: 4 'scared' verses visible on first load).
Confetti.js looks correct: pointer-events-none, z-[80], 2.6s auto-dismiss - cannot block clicks.

success_rate.frontend: 88% (7/8 features verified: 8 tiles render, prayer prompts/save/AI/delete, scriptures 8 cats + default verses + read-aloud, mood logs/stars increment, existing tile nav, PIN gate, teen signup. FAILED: confetti trigger.)

test_credentials: Demo parent parent@brighterdayz.org / Sunshine123, PIN 2468. Teen signup: any new <name>@example.com with age>=13 works.
seed_data_creation: Created 'TestKidConf' and 'ConfettiKid' child profiles under demo parent during testing (visible in Parent Dashboard screenshot). Also one TeenT teen account via API. Harmless test data; can be cleaned up via parent dashboard trash icon.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes KidHome.js pickMood to call setCelebrate(true) when badges count increases, retest by: (1) login parent@brighterdayz.org/Sunshine123, (2) create fresh child via add-child-btn, (3) click mood-struggling (or any mood), (4) within 2.6s verify DOM has div.fixed.pointer-events-none.z-[80] containing >0 <span> emoji children. Backend already validated. All other features pass.
rca of the issue: RCA: In /app/frontend/src/pages/KidHome.js, state setter setCelebrate is declared (line 30) and consumed by <Confetti show={celebrate}/> (line 54), but never invoked. pickMood() (lines 42-48) calls api.post /moods and only does setChild(data.child) without diffing badges. Backend response shape: {mood:{...}, child:{...badges:[...]}}. Reproduction: fresh child + any mood => stars 0->2, badges goes from [] to ['first_step'], no visual confetti. Mitigation: diff badges length pre/post and setCelebrate(true) if it grew.
